EIFS Contractor in Guadalupe County, TX

Star Stucco of Austin delivers certified EIFS installation and repair across Guadalupe County. From rapidly growing communities in Schertz and Cibolo to the established neighborhoods and commercial areas of Seguin, our team provides Exterior Insulation and Finish Systems built for the warm, humid conditions along the Guadalupe River corridor.

Get a Free EstimateCall (512) 706-9699

EIFS Designed for Guadalupe County's Growing Market

Guadalupe County sits at the eastern edge of the San Antonio metro, where the Guadalupe River cuts through rich bottomland and rolling prairies. The county's growth has accelerated in recent years, with Schertz and Cibolo among the fastest-expanding cities in the region. This development boom has created strong demand for exterior systems that deliver both energy efficiency and architectural distinction. EIFS meets that demand with continuous insulation that reduces cooling loads and finish options that help one project stand apart from the next.

The county's climate features long, hot summers with high humidity levels influenced by the Guadalupe River and Gulf moisture patterns. These conditions make moisture management a priority for any exterior cladding system. Modern drainage EIFS incorporates a water management layer behind the insulation that channels incidental moisture safely out of the wall assembly, providing an additional level of protection beyond what traditional stucco or siding can offer.

Our projects in Guadalupe County range from new residential installations in the Schertz-Cibolo growth corridor to commercial work in downtown Seguin and along the I-10 corridor. We understand the local building department process, soil conditions, and the humidity-driven moisture strategies that ensure EIFS installations last in this environment.

What's Included With Our EIFS Contractor in Guadalupe County

Every project includes expert craftsmanship, quality materials, and a commitment to lasting results.

EIFS System Evaluation

We evaluate the building substrate, humidity exposure, and site orientation to design an EIFS system engineered for Guadalupe County's warm, humid climate.

Insulation Board Installation

Continuous EPS insulation is bonded and fastened to the wall, reducing the thermal bridging that allows heat into buildings during the extended Guadalupe County summer.

Base Coat & Mesh Application

A polymer-modified base coat with fiberglass reinforcement creates the impact-resistant shell over the insulation, providing a durable substrate for the finish coat.

Finish Coat & Texturing

The acrylic finish is applied in your chosen color and texture, whether that is a contemporary profile for a Schertz development or a warmer, more traditional look for a Seguin property.

Sealant & Flashing Details

Windows, doors, roof transitions, and penetrations receive comprehensive sealant and flashing to manage the heavy rainfall and high humidity characteristic of the Guadalupe River corridor.

Final Inspection & Walkthrough

The entire system is inspected for adhesion, drainage performance, and finish consistency. We review maintenance practices suited to Guadalupe County's humidity and weather patterns.

Common EIFS Contractor Needs in Guadalupe County

Guadalupe County's growth and diverse building activity generate a wide range of EIFS project requests. These are the services our clients ask for most often.

EIFS Installation on New Construction

Builders in the Schertz, Cibolo, and Seguin markets specify EIFS for its energy performance and design versatility. We work with general contractors to integrate EIFS into the construction schedule for residential and commercial projects.

EIFS Repair & Patching

Hail, accidental damage, and minor settlement cracks require prompt EIFS repair to maintain the moisture barrier. We restore damaged sections with color- and texture-matched materials that blend with the existing wall.

Moisture Intrusion Remediation

Guadalupe County's river-influenced humidity can cause moisture issues in older EIFS without drainage. We diagnose the source, repair the substrate, install drainage components, and rebuild the system to current performance standards.

Foam Trim & Architectural Details

Custom EPS foam trim — window surrounds, cornices, banding, and cap moldings — adds character to Guadalupe County homes and commercial buildings without adding structural weight to foundations on the area's clay soils.

Commercial EIFS Systems

Retail, office, and hospitality properties along the I-10 corridor and in the Schertz-Cibolo commercial districts use EIFS for energy-compliant, attractive exteriors. We manage commercial installations with attention to business continuity.

EIFS-to-Stucco Conversion

Property owners who prefer to replace aging barrier EIFS with traditional stucco or a modern drainage EIFS rely on us for thorough removal, substrate evaluation, and quality re-cladding.

Why Choose Us for EIFS Contractor in Guadalupe County?

  • Certified applicators for Sto Corp, Dryvit, and Senergy/BASF EIFS products with full manufacturer warranty authorization
  • Deep understanding of Guadalupe County's river-influenced humidity and the moisture management it demands of exterior systems
  • Energy-focused approach that helps Seguin, Schertz, and Cibolo property owners reduce cooling expenses through superior insulation
  • Experienced with projects from single-family homes to large commercial developments along the I-10 and I-35 growth corridors
  • Manufacturer-backed warranties on all materials and installation labor when completed by our certified team
  • Detailed proposals, responsive communication, and reliable scheduling from first contact through project completion

Licensed & Insured

Full liability coverage and workers' compensation for every project in Guadalupe County.

20+ Years Experience

Decades of hands-on stucco work across Central Texas residential and commercial properties.

Certified Applicators

Certified by Sto Corp, Senergy/BASF, Master Wall, Dryvit, and Parex LaHabra for the highest quality results.

Our EIFS Contractor Process

A straightforward approach from start to finish — no surprises, no shortcuts.

1

Arrange Your EIFS Consultation

Call (512) 706-9699 to schedule an on-site assessment at your Guadalupe County property. We will evaluate the building, discuss your goals, and present the EIFS options that best suit your project.

2

System Evaluation & Design

We inspect substrates, evaluate humidity and moisture conditions, and select the EIFS components — insulation type, drainage system, base coat, mesh, and finish — that will deliver optimal performance.

3

Review Scope & Investment

A comprehensive written proposal covers scope, materials, timeline, and cost. We explain each component so you have a clear picture of the project before installation begins.

4

Professional Installation

Our certified installers complete the EIFS system to manufacturer standards, maintain a clean work environment, and conclude with a thorough inspection and final walkthrough.
